article_meta.title: Polling analyst Patrick Allocco told Newsmax on Monday that despite wide disparities in early surveys on U.S

reasoning: The model returned an empty array. The article is primarily composed of quoted material from Patrick Allocco, so most content is correctly excluded. However, there are some defensible bias instances the model could have flagged: (1) The article is essentially a single-source piece with no opposing viewpoint - a polling analyst from 'Zoose Political Index' whose perspective goes unchallenged. This could be flagged as Bias by Omission (no alternative polling analysts or political scientists are consulted). (2) The inclusion of the Newsmax promotional material at the end ('fastest-growing cable news channel', 'one of the top news brands') is self-promotional language in the author's prose that could be flagged. (3) The article's structural choice to present only one analyst's interpretation of polling disparities without any counterbalancing expert constitutes Slant. That said, some of these are debatable - the promotional content may not be 'article' content per se, and single-source articles aren't always biased. The empty array is partially defensible but misses some legitimate concerns.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model correctly avoided flagging any quoted material from Allocco, which constitutes the vast majority of the article. No attribution rule violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: The model returned an empty array, so there are zero false positives. Score of 1.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed some defensible bias instances: the single-source structure (Bias by Omission - no alternative analysts), and the self-promotional Newsmax content embedded in the article. The article is heavily quote-driven so there's limited author prose to analyze, but the structural choices and the promotional content are worth flagging.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No bias types were applied since the output was empty, so there are no incorrect labels. Score of 1 by default.
